methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $18M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Dendra Systems is a Series A-stage climate and energy company founded by Susan Graham and Irina Fedorenko, having raised $18M to date. Valuation analysis draws on a comparable set of 9 private companies and 1 public company (LNZA) operating in the same sector. Peer companies at Series A include Graphyte ($200M), Fourth Power ($150M), and Steady Energy ($110M), forming a median comparable valuation of $150M with a range of $110M–$200M.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $18M raised as a baseline proxy and applies a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A to next-stage transitions, implying a $36M valuation. Peer-median analysis directly references the three same-stage, same-sector comparables, yielding a $150M result. The discrepancy reflects differences in company maturity, capital efficiency, and market positioning relative to the median peer.

The triangulated valuation range is $31M (low), $93M (median), to $172M (high). This wide spread reflects meaningful uncertainty around Dendra's positioning within the peer cohort: stage-progression methods anchor conservatively on capital deployed, while peer comparables suggest substantially higher valuations for proven climate-tech models at this stage. Key uncertainties include the degree to which Dendra's technology roadmap and market traction justify peer-level multiples, and whether capital raised to date reflects proportional progress relative to Graphyte, Fourth Power, and Steady Energy at their respective Series A closings.
